title: adhatoda sulcata
intro: Adhatoda sulcata, also known as Vasa or Malabar nut, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and Africa. The leaves and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, exhibiting medicinal properties that include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Adhatoda sulcata include relief from respiratory issues, such as asthma and bronchitis, as well as potential benefits for digestive health and skin conditions. Adhatoda sulcata is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or powder, and is also used in traditional medicine to make various formulations, including decoctions and ointments.
